articles.title: Soldier's $400,000 Bet on Secret Raid Sparks Insider Trading Debate
articles.summary: A U.S. Special Forces soldier who helped plan the capture of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro was indicted for using classified information to bet on Polymarket. He won over $400,000. The case raises concerns about insider trading in prediction markets, where people bet on geopolitical events. President Trump said he's "not happy" about such betting. The White House warned staff against using non-public info for bets. Critics note Trump's son Don Jr. is an adviser to Polymarket and Kalshi. The indictment tests whether existing regulations apply to these markets.

articles.title: A Carousel's Second Spin: From Segregation to Smithsonian
articles.summary: After three years of restoration, the Smithsonian National Carousel reopened on the National Mall. But its history goes deeper than paint and polish. Originally at Gwynn Oak Amusement Park in Maryland, it was segregated until August 28, 1963 — the same day Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his "I Have a Dream" speech. That day, 11-month-old Sharon Langley became the first Black child to ride. Now 63, she rode again on a horse named "Freedom Rider." The carousel's journey from a segregated park to a national monument symbolizes the civil rights struggle. Kids today just enjoy the ride — one called it "the fastest carousel I've been on."

articles.title: US and Iran Ship Seizures Violate International Law, Shipping Body Warns
articles.summary: The International Chamber of Shipping, representing 80% of the world's merchant fleet, says both the US and Iran are violating international law by capturing commercial ships. Iran seized two vessels — MSC Francesca and Epaminondas — while the US captured the Iran-linked Majestic X. The group calls the seizures a political act that endangers innocent seafarers. About 20,000 sailors are stranded in the Gulf due to the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz, driving up global fuel prices. The shipping body demands respect for freedom of navigation and the release of all crews.
